data.title: As a Java developer, validate.sh warns when commands.security is not configured
data.description:
## Description

**What**: [STRETCH — not required for epic closure] Add commands.security as a new optional config key to CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md. Update validate.sh to emit [DSO WARN] commands.security not configured and exit 0 (non-blocking) when the key is absent. Document Java defaults: mvn org.owasp:dependency-check-maven:check -DfailBuildOnCVSS=7 (Maven) and ./gradlew dependencyCheckAnalyze (Gradle).

**Scope**:
- IN: CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md: add commands.security key with Java defaults; validate.sh: add non-blocking warning when key absent; Java-specific OWASP defaults documented
- OUT: Actually running the security scan (that's commands.security's job when configured); stretch — not sprint-blocking

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md documents commands.security with Java Maven and Gradle default values
  ← Satisfies: "[STRETCH] commands.security config key added to CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md"
- When this story is complete, validate.sh emits [DSO WARN] commands.security not configured and exits 0 when commands.security is absent from dso-config.conf
  ← Satisfies: "validate.sh emits [DSO WARN] with exit 0 when absent"

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Exit code must remain 0 — this is a warning, not a blocking failure
- [Scope] This is STRETCH — skip if sprint capacity is constrained; epic closure does not require this story

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
